codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 5 findings: (1) missing AC for staged-new-file-in-files_created exclusion from D1 reverse check; (2) missing acceptance-criteria JSON artifact; (3) no files_created under-reporting check (D1 inverse for created files); (4) internal inconsistency: R1 table says 'absent from files_modified' but M1 says 'absent from files_modified ∪ files_created'; (5) OBSERVATION_ONLY: D2 QA-skip consequence is conditional, not guaranteed
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Findings 1 and 4 are blocking spec defects and are raised as issues. Finding 2 is raised as non-blocking advisory. Finding 3 is classified out-of-scope (beyond user requirement). Finding 5 is observation-only and does not affect the verdict.

D1_under_reporting_gap_is_real.evidence: agents/qa.md lines 411-422 confirmed via direct Read: iterates dev.files_modified against git diff --name-only (over-report direction only). Step 4 fires for every path in dev.files_modified absent from the diff. No step iterates git diff output against dev.files_modified. Under-reporting direction is entirely absent.

D2_aggregate_missing_fields.evidence: commands/dev.md lines 720-734 confirmed via direct Read: aggregate construction rule lists exactly 8 fields (dev.status, dev.tasks_completed, dev.scripts_created, dev.permissions_to_add, dev.files_modified, dev.files_created, blocking_issues, recommendations). baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, and dev.observed_preexisting are all absent. Example JSON at lines 739-756 also confirmed missing these three fields. Line 699 summary bullet likewise omits them.

D3_staged_files_missed.evidence: agents/dev.md:495 confirmed via direct Read: files_created derivation uses ONLY 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard'. agents/qa.md:427 confirmed to mirror the same command. Neither line mentions 'git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A'. Git semantics confirmed: a staged file (git add) has an index entry and does NOT appear in --others output, only in --cached --diff-filter=A.

files_created_exclusion_in_D1_handled.evidence: M1 correctly specifies comparison against 'dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created'. S1 and Risk 1 explicitly note overlap. However, no AC tests the specific boundary condition: diff contains a staged-new file that appears in files_created (not files_modified), and the reverse check must NOT fire for it. AC1's GIVEN uses dev.files_created=[] (or omits it), making the union-exclusion path untestable from AC1. AC5 tests the observed_preexisting boundary but not the files_created union path. This is a real AC gap (see issues).

D1_approach_correct.rationale: D1 fix (compare diff_files against files_modified ∪ files_created, subtract baseline_dirty_paths parsed from porcelain) is technically correct. The git diff --name-only command captures all tracked-file changes including added staged files. Using the union prevents false positives on staged new files. Excluding baseline_dirty_paths (not observed_preexisting) is correct per agents/dev.md:499 definition.

D2_approach_correct.rationale: Adding baseline_head_sha and baseline_dirty_snapshot as equality-checked scalars (not unioned) is correct — these are dispatch-owned scalars copied verbatim by workers (agents/dev.md:503). Adding dev.observed_preexisting as UNION of per-worker lists is correct — it is a list field that is worker-owned (agents/dev.md:521). Blocking on mismatch (not warning) is correct for provenance integrity. Requiring both summary and detailed edit locations is correct per Risk 4.

D3_approach_correct.rationale: UNION of git ls-files --others --exclude-standard (untracked files) and git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A (staged new files), minus baseline_dirty_paths, is the correct combined derivation. Codex independently verified the git semantics by running a test in a temp repo (output confirmed in codex session): staged-new.txt appears in diff --cached --diff-filter=A but NOT in ls-files --others; untracked-new.txt appears in ls-files --others but NOT in diff --cached. The union captures both cases correctly.

issues.title: Missing AC for files_created-as-union-exclusion boundary in D1 reverse check
issues.description: The spec requires the D1 reverse check to compare diff_files against dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created (M1, S1, Risk 1). However, no AC tests the boundary case where a path is in the diff AND in files_created but NOT in files_modified. AC1's GIVEN uses dev.files_created absent or implicitly empty, testing only the case where files_modified contains the path. AC5 tests the observed_preexisting boundary, not the files_created union path. Without a concrete AC for this boundary, an implementer could test only AC1 and AC5 and still implement the reverse check against files_modified alone — satisfying all ACs while missing the union requirement.
issues.proposed_fix: Add AC6: GIVEN a dev-report with baseline_head_sha set, dev.files_modified=[], dev.files_created=['staged-new.py'], and baseline_dirty_snapshot=''; WHEN git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> returns 'staged-new.py'; THEN QA does NOT raise files_modified_underreport_violation for staged-new.py (it is present in files_created, which satisfies the union check). This tests the union-exclusion path that M1 and S1 require but no existing AC exercises.

issues.title: Internal inconsistency: R1 acceptance criterion says 'absent from files_modified' but M1 says 'absent from files_modified ∪ files_created'
issues.description: The requirements_decomposition table R1 acceptance criterion (ticket line 97) reads: 'QA flags under-reporting: a file in git diff but absent from files_modified (and not in baseline_dirty) raises files_modified_underreport_violation'. This omits the files_created side of the union. M1 (ticket line 74) correctly specifies 'absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created'. A developer implementing the R1 criterion text literally would check only files_modified, missing the union. The AC text must be the authoritative testable statement — if R1 and M1 conflict, a developer following R1 will produce an incorrect implementation that passes R1's criterion but fails the actual requirement.
issues.location: ticket-20260524-125300-D.md R1 table row (line 97) vs M1 (line 74)
issues.proposed_fix: Update the R1 acceptance_criterion column to read: 'QA flags under-reporting: a file in git diff but absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created (and not in baseline_dirty_paths) raises files_modified_underreport_violation (severity: critical)'. Mirror this change in context-20260524-125300-D.json requirements_decomposition[0].acceptance_criterion.

issues.title: acceptance_criteria_path in context JSON points to a file that does not exist
issues.description: context-20260524-125300-D.json line 240 declares acceptance_criteria_path: 'docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-D.json'. This file does not exist (confirmed by Codex: ls output showed the file is absent; only -B, -C, and -push variants exist). The ACs are fully specified in the ticket .md file, so this does not block verification, but the stale artifact pointer could confuse downstream tooling that reads acceptance_criteria_path to locate ACs.
issues.proposed_fix: Either create docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-20260524-125300-D.json with the structured AC list matching the ticket .md ACs, or set acceptance_criteria_path to null in the context JSON to indicate the ACs live in the ticket file. Include the new AC6 (from I1) if the file is created.

out_of_scope_observations.observation: D1 reverse check covers files_modified under-reporting (via git diff --name-only, which includes tracked changes). It does not add a symmetric files_created under-reporting check (i.e., a file that appears as untracked/staged new but is absent from dev.files_created). The user requirement for D1 stated only that 'files in the git diff that are NOT in files_modified' should be caught — the diff-based check was the user's scope. A files_created_underreport_violation check is a defensible extension but is not part of the user's stated requirement. Raising as observation only.

Codex notes that QA's skip-on-missing-baseline_head_sha is not guaranteed for parallel cycles because QA can also read baseline_head_sha from context JSON. The D2 impact description's phrase 'provenance check is skipped' is conditional, not absolute. The fix requirement remains correct (aggregate must carry the field), but the impact phrasing in the spec slightly overstates the skip consequence. Non-blocking phrasing concern.

evidence_quality.notes: All three bugs are backed by direct file reads with line numbers confirmed. Codex independently verified D3 git semantics by running an actual test in a temp repo. The BA's claims are evidence-backed, not asserted.

scope_alignment.notes: BA scope correctly matches the three user-stated gaps. files_created_underreport_violation (Codex finding 3) is correctly excluded as beyond the user requirement. changelog-analyst.md mirror is correctly logged as path-external out-of-scope observation.

investigation_completeness.notes: Gaps I1 and I2 above. The investigation correctly identified all three bugs and their locations. However, AC coverage has a gap (I1: no AC for the files_created union-exclusion path) and the R1 table text contradicts M1 (I2: inconsistent acceptance criterion text). These are investigation artifacts, not missing investigation steps.

affected_file_accuracy.notes: All three affected files exist and contain exactly the lines BA cited. agents/qa.md:411-422, agents/qa.md:427-438, commands/dev.md:699,720-734,739-756, agents/dev.md:495 all verified via direct Read. The changelog-analyst.md out-of-scope observation is also accurate (line 127 confirmed to use --others only).

spec_text_vs_execution_drift.notes: I2: R1 table acceptance criterion text ('absent from files_modified') does not match M1 literal text ('absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created'). A literal implementation of the R1 AC text would fail to implement the union check. This is a spec_text_vs_execution_drift pattern between the requirements_decomposition acceptance criterion and the Must Have requirement text.

qa.approval_notes: REJECTED. Two blocking issues require BA resolution before dev proceeds: (I1) add a dedicated AC (AC6) testing the staged-new-file-in-files_created boundary case for the D1 reverse check; (I2) fix the R1 table acceptance criterion to say 'absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created' matching M1. One non-blocking advisory (I3): resolve or null the stale acceptance_criteria_path pointer. The three bugs (D1/D2/D3) are real and correctly characterized. Fix approaches are technically correct. Once I1 and I2 are resolved, the spec should be approved.
